- Order tenure of 5 years
- Production starts from Q1 FY28

Technology Advantage
- Powered by proprietary NexTrac platform
- Advanced software-controlled torque management
- Electromechanical actuation capabilities

Strategic Significance
- OEM's first FWD-based AWD platform

- Strengthens position in premium SUV segment

Key Takeaway
- Long-term order enhances revenue visibility
- Validates Divgi's AWD technology capabilities
- Positive for growth in the drivetrain business

RBI MPC Minutes: Key Highlights

- Preference remains for a wait-and-watch approach
- CPI inflation may peak near 5.9% in Q3
- Fuel and food expected to drive inflation higher
- Tight financial conditions reduce need for rate hikes
- Monitoring West Asia conflict and macro impact closely
- Economy remains resilient despite geopolitical risks
- Inflation vigilance remains a key priority
- Clean energy transition seen as strategically important

Key Takeaway
- RBI remains cautious but not immediately hawkish
- No urgent indication of further rate hikes
- Focus remains on inflation trajectory and global risks

Impact of Telegram ban🙃 -

VPN demand in India seeing a sharp spike

Downloads of major VPN apps rose 49%, moving from a recent daily average of 139,000 to 208,000.

Proton VPN downloads on Apple’s App Store in India jumped 113%, while Turbo VPN downloads rose 85%.

On Google Play, Proton VPN downloads were up 64%, while Turbo VPN downloads increased 35%.

This surge also pushed VPN apps higher in India’s app store rankings

Whenever there is uncertainty, restrictions, privacy concerns or access related issues, VPN demand tends to spike quickly

Jio completes 10 years of stellar success. Reliance Retail completes 20 years as a trailblazer. In both businesses, we remain the unbeatable Number One. When a patriotic, high-performance company consistently serves the aspirations of nearly 1.5 billion Indians, there is no limit to what it can achieve: Mukesh D. Ambani

According to the latest WIPO report, Jio Platforms has jumped from rank 340 to 20 in just one year in innovation velocity through patents - the only Indian company in the top 20 globally. Our brilliant team of scientists, engineers and innovators have made India proud: Mukesh D. Ambani

Maximum energy self-sufficiency and AI self-sufficiency must become our national missions. The success of these missions is critical to the success of VIKSIT BHARAT. Reliance is playing a leading role in both national imperatives: Mukesh D. Ambani

Alembic Pharma Receives USFDA Tentative Approval

Regulatory Milestone
- Received USFDA tentative approval
- For Binimetinib Tablets 45 mg
- Generic equivalent of Mektovi®

Therapeutic Use
- Used with encorafenib
- Treats metastatic melanoma
- Also indicated for BRAF-mutated NSCLC

Competitive Position
- Sole first applicant for 45 mg strength
- Filed with Paragraph IV certification
- Strong competitive advantage

Market Opportunity
- Potential 180-day exclusivity
- US market size around $259 Mn
- Attractive oncology opportunity

Strategic Significance
- Strengthens oncology portfolio
- Expands US generics presence
- Enhances complex product pipeline

Key Takeaway
- Important US regulatory achievement
- Potential exclusivity could support profitability
- Positive for US generics and oncology growth strategy

Negative For Shrimp Stocks 

Andhra Pradesh’s shrimp farmers can’t catch a break: New crisis looms after tariff shock

Months after the US tariff shock, shrimp farmers in Andhra Pradesh are facing renewed financial pressure from a hike in aqua feed prices, significantly increasing production costs and prompting threats of protests.

According to industry estimates, feed maker companies have increased prices by Rs 12-16 per kg with effect from June 13. The hike comes at a time when shrimp farmers are still recovering from the disruptions and losses caused by the US tariffs.

Farmers operating aqua ponds, which require thousands of kilograms of feed every week, are now feeling the pinch.

📢 Company Update | YAAP Digital Appoints Sambit Mohanty as Group Chief Creative Officer

🔹 YAAP Digital (NSE: YAAP) has appointed Sambit Mohanty as Group Chief Creative Officer (GCCO), reinforcing its leadership team as the Company continues to scale its integrated marketing and digital capabilities.

🔹 A seasoned industry professional with over two decades of experience, Sambit joins YAAP from McCann Worldgroup India, where he served as Creative Head – North & South.

🔹 The appointment reflects YAAP's continued investment in high-calibre leadership talent and its commitment to building a world-class, technology-enabled marketing enterprise.

NUVAMA SAYS - Only 2 Vedanta stocks to be excluded today 

Update (Nifty Next 50 Rejig) - Vedanta Power & Vedanta Iron & Steel Exclusions are Today (Announcement)
 
* As per the index methodology, Vedanta Power Ltd. and Vedanta Iron & Steel Ltd. did not hit the lower price band on two consecutive trading days (June 16 and June 17, 2026). Accordingly, NSE Indices has decided to exclude both stocks from the relevant indices effective June 19, 2026 (post close of June 18, 2026). Thus adjustment will be today in last thirty minutes.
 
* It may be noted that even if either stock hits the lower price band on June 18, 2026, the exclusion will not be deferred further.
 
* The remaining two entities, Vedanta Aluminium and Vedanta Oil & Gas, are yet to be excluded from the Nifty Next 50. We expect these exclusions to take place over the next few days. We have also highlighted the potential passive outflows associated with both entities.

India Battery Energy Storage

Key Finding
- India lost 2.1 TWh renewable power in FY26
- Renewable energy curtailed to support coal operations
- Represents 1.3% of renewable generation
- Estimated value loss: ₹629 Cr

Why Curtailment Happened
- Coal plants operating at Minimum Technical Load (MTL)
- Coal units generally cannot reduce below ~55% capacity
- Excess solar generation forces renewable curtailment
- Structural grid flexibility challenge emerging

Storage Requirement
- India needs ~10 GWh battery storage immediately
- Storage can absorb excess daytime solar power
- Reduces renewable energy wastage
- Improves grid balancing capability

Solar Growth
- 24 GW solar capacity added between Oct 2025–Apr 2026
- Total installed solar capacity reaches ~154 GW
- Renewable growth outpacing grid flexibility
- Storage deployment lagging generation additions

Grid Stress Indicators
- Solar and wind reached 41% of midday generation
- Coal generation required massive ramping cycles
- Curtailment becoming a regular balancing tool
- Renewable down-regulation increasing rapidly

Worsening Trend
- Renewable curtailment exceeds 3,600 GWh by June 2026
- More than 1,400 GWh added since March 2026
- Curtailment volumes accelerating sharply
- No longer an isolated event

Who Benefits
Battery Storage
- Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS)
- Grid-scale storage developers
- Battery manufacturers
- Energy management providers

Power Equipment
- BESS integrators
- Inverter manufacturers
- Transformer suppliers
- Grid modernization companies

Renewable Ecosystem
- Solar developers
- Hybrid renewable projects
- Round-the-clock renewable providers
- Dispatchable clean energy operators

Policy Implications
- Faster BESS tenders likely
- Storage-linked renewable projects may accelerate
- Grid flexibility becomes policy priority
- Energy security benefits increase

Investment Theme
- India is transitioning from renewable generation buildout
- Next phase focuses on storage and grid flexibility
- Batteries becoming critical infrastructure
- Storage likely to see multi-year investment cycle

Key Monitorables
- BESS auction pipeline
- Storage project commissioning
- Battery manufacturing capacity additions
- Renewable curtailment trends
- Grid modernization initiatives

Key Takeaway
- India's renewable challenge is no longer generation capacity but grid flexibility
- ₹629 Cr worth of renewable electricity was lost in FY26 because storage infrastructure has not kept pace with solar growth
- The need for 10 GWh of immediate battery storage highlights that BESS is rapidly becoming the next critical investment theme in India's energy transition

Emerald Finance Limited collaborates with Bizvisory HR Global Solutions to deliver Early Wage Access.

Emerald Finance Limited has partnered with Bizvisory Hr Global Solutions Private Limited, Delhi to launch our new Early-Wage-Access (EWA) program.

This strategic initiative is designed to provide immediate financial flexibility  towards workforce through compensation access. Employees can draw down on their earned salary in real-time to cover day-to-day needs, with automated settlement during the regular monthly payroll.

Key Program Highlights:
* On-Demand Liquidity: Instant access to accrued earnings to match real-time cash demands.
* Sub-Cycle Financial Relief: Mitigates mid-month working capital pressures for employees.
* Automated Operational Flow: Features an integrated, tech-driven settlement process for streamlined, end-to-end payroll recovery.

This partnership accelerates Emerald Finance Limited’s expansion into high-yield, employer-linked salary advance solutions. It broadens retail financial services portfolio while equipping the workforce with a responsible, flexible liquidity tool.

Supreme Power Equipment Limited Continues Its Order Winning Momentum with New ₹11.40 Cr Orders

The Company has secured two orders aggregating to approximately ₹11.40 Cr from a company situated in Karnataka for the supply of power transformers. 

Order Highlights :
* Client: Company situated in Karnataka
* Scope: Supply of 20 MVA, 110/33–11 kV Power Transformers
* Order Value: ₹11.40 Cr (approx.)
* Orders: Two separate orders of ₹5.70 Cr each
* Execution Timeline: Within 12 months (approx.)

The new orders further strengthen the Company's order book and reinforce its growing presence in the southern region.

Unihealth Hospitals Limited (NSE: UNIHEALTH) – Key Healthcare Milestone

Unihealth Hospitals Limited announced UMC Hospitals, Navi Mumbai completed its first IVUS-Guided Coronary Intervention.

The procedure introduces intravascular imaging technology, enabling precision-driven cardiac interventions with improved assessment and stent optimization.

This milestone strengthens UMC Hospitals’ cardiac platform and reflects focus on advanced medical infrastructure and technology-led healthcare delivery.

For FY26, Unihealth Hospitals reported Total Income ₹137.01 Cr, EBITDA ₹58.82 Cr and Net Profit ₹25.83 Cr.

SONA COMSTAR : APPROVES ₹62.6 CR CAPEX FOR ADVANCED ROBOTICS BUSINESS 🤖⚙️

• 🚀 Sona Comstar has approved a capital expenditure (Capex) of ₹62.6 crore (₹626 million) to enter the robotics industry, including advanced robotics. 

• 🤖 The new business line will focus on the manufacturing of components and systems for advanced robotics, marking a strategic diversification beyond the automotive sector. 

• 🌐 The initiative is expected to provide access to new end markets and application areas outside the automotive industry. 

• 📈 The company expects the move to broaden its product portfolio and create long-term growth opportunities. 

• 🏭 The investment was approved by the Board based on the recommendation of the Capex Committee. 

• 👨‍💼 The Board also appointed Mr. Pankaj Gupta as Senior Vice President (Legal), Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective June 17, 2026. 

• 🔄 Ms. Suman Poddar and Mr. Arjun Singh resigned from their interim roles as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, respectively, following the appointment of a permanent appointee.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (Sona Comstar’s entry into advanced robotics diversifies its business beyond automotive, opens new growth avenues and strengthens its positioning in emerging technologies.)
OIL FALLS 4 % , TO NEAR $ 80 MARK

SENCO GOLD : ICRA UPGRADES CREDIT RATING TO A+ / A1 ⭐💎

• 📈 ICRA has upgraded Senco Gold’s credit ratings by one notch, reflecting improved business performance and credit profile. 

• 🏦 The company’s ₹2,400 crore working capital facilities have been upgraded to [ICRA] A+ (Stable) / A1 from [ICRA] A (Stable) / A2+. 

• 💰 The ₹495 crore Fixed Deposit Programme has also been upgraded to [ICRA] A+ (Stable) from [ICRA] A (Stable). 

• 📊 ICRA highlighted Senco’s strong growth, with consolidated revenue rising to ₹8,430 crore in FY26, while PAT surged to ₹569 crore. 

• 🏬 Senco currently operates 116 company-owned stores and 85 franchise stores, including two international stores in Dubai. 

• 🌍 The company continues to expand beyond West Bengal to strengthen its pan-India presence, reducing geographical concentration risk over time. 

• 📈 ICRA expects Senco’s revenue to grow by 18–20% in FY27, supported by store additions, rising gold prices and growth in non-gold jewellery. 

• ⚠️ Key risks highlighted include high working capital requirements, volatility in gold prices and intense competition in the jewellery retail segment. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The credit rating upgrade reflects stronger financial performance, improved profitability and enhanced creditworthiness, which could support lower borrowing costs and future expansion.)

Unihealth Hospitals Limited Announces Strategic Share Swap to Increase Stake in Victoria Hospital Limited, Uganda

Unihealth Hospitals Limited (NSE: UNIHEALTH) has announced a strategic share-swap transaction to increase its ownership stake in Victoria Hospital Limited, Uganda, further strengthening its international healthcare platform and enhancing long-term shareholder value.

Key Transaction Highlights:

• Unihealth currently holds 50% equity ownership in Victoria Hospital Limited and proposes to acquire additional equity from existing shareholders through a share-swap arrangement, subject to applicable approvals and customary closing conditions.

• The transaction will enable Unihealth to increase strategic control and participate more significantly in the future growth of Victoria Hospital, a key healthcare asset in Uganda.

Strategic & Financial Benefits for Unihealth Shareholders:

✅ Enhanced Earnings Potential: Greater ownership participation in a profitable and growing healthcare asset is expected to improve earnings visibility and enhance consolidated profitability attributable to Unihealth shareholders.

✅ Value Creation Through Ownership Consolidation: The increased stake strengthens alignment of strategic objectives, enables deeper operational integration and provides greater participation in Victoria Hospital’s future expansion opportunities.

✅ Capital Efficient Growth: The share-swap structure allows Unihealth to expand its international healthcare footprint without significant immediate cash outflow, preserving liquidity for further growth initiatives.

✅ Strong Underlying Asset Performance: Victoria Hospital delivered robust performance in FY 2025-26, with revenue of ₹114.47 crore compared to ₹85.99 crore in the previous year and profit after tax of ₹43.63 crore compared to ₹25.81 crore, reflecting strong operational execution and profitability.

Victoria Hospital operates the 120-bedded UMC Victoria Hospital in Kampala, and the 30-bedded UMC Hospital in Entebbe, Uganda. The hospitals are tertiary care facilities, offering advanced clinical services across multiple specialties including orthopaedics, spine, nephrology, oncology, cardiology, gasteroenterology, urology, general and laparoscopic surgery, dialysis, diagnostics, IVF, paediatrics and critical care.

The transaction represents another important step in Unihealth’s strategy of building a scalable healthcare platform across emerging markets, leveraging established hospital infrastructure, clinical expertise and regional partnerships to drive sustainable growth.

Unihealth remains focused on creating long-term value for shareholders through disciplined expansion, operational excellence and strategic growth initiatives.

GARWARE HI-TECH FILMS : DGTR RECOMMENDS ANTI-DUMPING DUTY ON CHINESE TPU PPF 🇮🇳🛡️

• 🏛️ The DGTR has recommended the imposition of anti-dumping duty on imports of TPU-based Paint Protection Film (PPF) originating from China. 

• 📄 The recommendation follows an investigation initiated based on Garware Hi-Tech Films’ application on behalf of the domestic industry. 

• ⚖️ The proposed duty will be equal to the lower of the dumping margin and injury margin, aimed at protecting domestic manufacturers. 

• 🏭 Garware Hi-Tech Films is a manufacturer of TPU-based PPF and expects to benefit from the DGTR findings. 

• 🇨🇳 The investigation covered imports of Thermoplastic Polyurethane (TPU)-based surface/Paint Protection Film from the People’s Republic of China. 

• ⏳ The final notification from the Central Government is still awaited, after which the duty may become effective. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Anti-dumping duty can curb low-cost Chinese imports, improve pricing power and support market share gains for Garware Hi-Tech Films.)

WELSPUN LIVING : FULLY COMMISSIONS US PILLOW MANUFACTURING UNIT 🇺🇸🛏️

• 🏭 Welspun Living’s subsidiary Welspun USA Inc. has fully commenced commercial production at its pillow manufacturing unit in Nevada, USA. 

• 📅 The unit became fully operational from June 15, 2026 (US Pacific Time). 

• 💰 The company had approved a US$13 million capex in July 2025 to set up this facility. 

• 🚀 The plant is aimed at expanding Welspun’s home textiles portfolio in the US market, particularly in the pillow category. 

• ⚙️ The company had already commenced partial commercial production at the facility from March 31, 2026. 

• 🌎 The full commissioning strengthens Welspun’s local manufacturing footprint in the US, enhancing supply chain efficiency and customer responsiveness. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Full commercialization of the Nevada facility expands capacity, strengthens US presence and supports growth in the higher-margin home textiles segment.)

ADANI PORTS : EXPANDS AI PARTNERSHIP WITH US-BASED KALERIS 🚢🤖

• 🤝 Adani Ports has expanded its partnership with US-based Kaleris to deploy an AI-augmented operating platform across 15 container terminals spanning 9 ports. 

• 💰 APSEZ plans to invest USD 850 million in technology and decarbonisation by 2031, including up to USD 100 million with Kaleris for automation and optimization. 

• 📈 The company expects to unlock 91 MMT of additional capacity (~10% of installed capacity) by 2030 through this initiative. 

• ⚙️ The AI platform is expected to improve RTG crane productivity by up to 20% and terminal truck productivity by up to 14%. 

• 🌐 Kaleris’ N4 Terminal Operating System will be deployed to enhance yard utilization, vessel turnaround and cargo movement efficiency. 

• 🚚 APSEZ currently handles 27% of India’s port volumes with a capacity of 653 MTPA, targeting 1 billion tonnes throughput by 2030. 

• 🧠 The initiative strengthens APSEZ’s AI-led logistics ecosystem spanning ports, rail, roads, warehouses and cargo gateways. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The AI-driven transformation can improve operational efficiency, unlock additional capacity and strengthen APSEZ’s leadership in integrated logistics.)

INOX WIND : SIGNS MoU TO SUPPLY 1,500 MW WIND TURBINES 🌬️⚡

• 🤝 Inox Wind has signed an MoU with Inox Clean Energy to supply 1,500 MW of wind turbines for renewable energy projects across India. 

• ⚙️ The supply will include Inox Wind’s advanced 3.3 MW and 4X MW wind turbine platforms. 

• 📈 The company’s order book will increase from 3.1 GW to over 4.5 GW, providing multi-year revenue visibility. 

• 🌱 The MoU supports the INOXGFL Group’s “One Integrated” renewable strategy, spanning manufacturing, EPC, IPP and O&M services. 

• 🚀 Inox Clean plans to add over 3 GW of renewable capacity annually and targets an operational portfolio of 14 GW by FY29. 

• 🌬️ Around 20–30% of Inox Clean’s annual capacity additions are expected to be wind, ensuring recurring order opportunities for Inox Wind. 

• 🏭 Inox Wind currently has a manufacturing capacity of ~2.5 GW per annum and offers end-to-end wind energy solutions. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The 1,500 MW MoU significantly enhances order visibility, strengthens intra-group synergies and supports long-term growth in India's renewable energy sector.)

INFONATIVE SOLUTIONS : EMPANELLED BY NATIONAL HOUSING BANK 🏦📄

• 🏛️ Infonative Solutions has been selected and empanelled as a Content Creator by the National Housing Bank (NHB). 

• 📜 The empanelment is pursuant to NHB’s tender dated March 17, 2026, for content creation activities. 

• 📩 The company received the formal communication from NHB on June 11, 2026. 

• 🎯 The empanelment enables Infonative to undertake activities specified under NHB’s content creation framework. 

• 🏢 NHB is a key institution in India’s housing finance ecosystem, potentially enhancing Infonative’s credibility and future business opportunities. 

• 🤝 The empanelment may open avenues for government and institutional assignments going forward. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Association with NHB strengthens Infonative’s credentials and could lead to future business opportunities from government and financial institutions.)

PINE LABS : DENIES REPORT ON GIFT CARD INCOME IMPACT 🚫💳

• 📰 Pine Labs has termed the media report titled “Inside Pine Labs’ profit story: The gift card income stream set to take a hit” as speculative, incorrect and misleading. 

• 🤝 The company clarified that its regulated products largely operate through co-branded program structures, where any breakage income belongs to partner brands, not Pine Labs. 

• 💰 Pine Labs stated that breakage income has never been a material part of its revenue or profit pool. 

• 📊 The company emphasized that such income has never been recognized in its P&L, and hence does not contribute meaningfully to earnings. 

• 🏦 Even if the RBI issues fresh regulations regarding breakage income, Pine Labs does not foresee any meaningful impact on its business, revenue or profitability. 

• ⏳ The co-branded operating model has been in place for over a decade across Pine Labs’ regulated programs in India. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The clarification removes concerns over potential regulatory risks and reassures investors that any RBI action is unlikely to materially impact Pine Labs’ financials.)

TT LIMITED : 8 LAKH WARRANTS LAPSE; ₹2.44 CR FORFEITED 📉💰

• ⚠️ TT Limited announced the lapse of 8,00,000 convertible warrants issued on a preferential basis after holders failed to exercise them within the stipulated period. 

• 💸 The company has forfeited ₹2.44 crore, representing the 25% upfront subscription amount paid at the time of allotment. 

• 📅 The warrants, allotted on December 16, 2024, had an 18-month tenure ending on June 15, 2026. 

• 🚫 Warrant holders failed to pay the remaining 75% consideration, leading to the lapse of all warrants. 

• 👥 VASM Consultants Pvt Ltd (2 lakh warrants) and Subhash Phootarmal Rathod (6 lakh warrants) were the affected warrant holders. 

• 📊 The company clarified that there will be no change in paid-up share capital, while the forfeited amount will be retained in accordance with accounting standards. 

🟢 Impact: Slightly Positive (The forfeiture adds ₹2.44 crore to the company without equity dilution, though the lapse indicates investors chose not to infuse additional capital.)

GRASIM : INAUGURATES INDIA'S LARGEST CPVC RESIN PLANT AT VILAYAT 🏭🧪

• 🏭 Grasim Industries has inaugurated Phase 1 of its CPVC resin plant at Vilayat, Gujarat, in collaboration with Lubrizol Advanced Materials India Pvt Ltd. 

• 📦 The plant has a planned capacity of 50,000 metric tonnes per annum (MTPA). 

• 🌍 The project is part of the world’s largest single-site CPVC resin facility with an overall planned capacity of 100,000 MTPA. 

• 🔗 Grasim had earlier signed agreements with Lubrizol for local production of CPVC resin in India, reducing import dependence. 

• ▶️ The company stated that commercial production commencement will be announced in due course. 

• 🇮🇳 The facility strengthens India's position in specialty chemicals and supports the growing demand from pipes, plumbing and infrastructure sectors. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The plant boosts Grasim’s specialty chemicals portfolio, enhances import substitution and opens up a large growth opportunity in the CPVC segment.)

DOWNGRADE TO UNDERPERFORM , TARGET PRICE 10,400

Recent rally on expected approvals seen in shares of Dixon Technologies is "overdone." 

Shares of Dixon Tech have rallied 24% from the March 30 closing levels of ₹9,637. Since then, the stock has gained 15% in April, and 3% each in May and June so far. 

Brokerage expects Dixon Tech's organic volumes to decline in financial year 2027 as it expects elevated memory prices to have a disproportionate impact on the Indian smartphone volumes.

Further, a delay in backward integration projects could also pose downside risks to estimates for the stock, according to CLSA, who added that with domestic smartphone market share plateauing, it expects growth to decelerate over the medium-term.

At 46 times financial year 2028 estimated price-to-earnings, CLSA finds Dixon's risk-reward to be unfavourable

SUVEN LIFE SCIENCES COMPLETES PHASE-1 STUDY OF CNS DRUG SUVN-16107 🧠💊

• ✅ Successfully completed First-in-Human (FIH) Phase-1 clinical study of SUVN-16107, a Muscarinic M1 Positive Allosteric Modulator (M1-PAM)

• 🚀 The drug candidate has now advanced into Phase-2 clinical development

• 🛡️ Study demonstrated a favourable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities or serious adverse events

• 🎯 Achieved projected therapeutic exposure in plasma and at the target site

• 🧬 Biomarker assessments showed evidence of central nervous system (CNS) activity and enhanced brain function

• 🧑‍⚕️ Pharmacokinetic assessments found no clinically meaningful differences between male and female participants

• 🍽️ Food intake had no clinically relevant impact on the drug's pharmacokinetics, supporting flexible dosing

• 🔬 SUVN-16107 is the fifth internally discovered NCE by Suven to enter clinical development, strengthening its CNS pipeline

• 📚 Detailed study findings are expected to be presented at medical conferences and peer-reviewed journals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Successful Phase-1 completion and progression to Phase-2 de-risks the asset and enhances the value of Suven's CNS pipeline.)

*JPM on L&T*
OW, TP Raised to Rs 5,060 from Rs4,570
L&T has demonstrated solid delivery under strategic plan Lakshya -26 (2021-2026).
Under plan, L&T:
1) has shown a 20% CAGR in its order inflows vs. target of 14%;
2) revenue at a 16% CAGR vs. target of 15%;
3) consol. ROE of 16.6% in FY26, up from 10% in FY21 (target of 18%).
L&T enters current 5-year strategic plan (Lakshya -31 over 2026-2031) building on this success - aiming to deliver:
1) a 10%-12% CAGR in order inflow; and,
2) a revenue CAGR of 12-15% over FY26-FY31 with 16%-17% consolidated ROE.
With re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz, tail risk to FY27 guidance has been removed
FY27 guidance given by L&T is healthy with 10-12% YoY growth in OI and Revenue and flat margins.
In addition, L&T enjoys optionality from: 1) an increase in capex in oil and gas in ME (e.g. UAE exiting OPEC, additional pipelines and storage infra, etc.); and, 2) acceleration in core infrastructure capex as re-construction opportunities in the Middle East materialize

*CITI on Tata Cons*
Buy, TP Rs 1450
Management meet Key takeaways
(a) Demand remains resilient, with no visible moderation so far.
(b) Growth outlook remains strong, led by sustained momentum in growth businesses (Sampann, RTD/NourishCo, Organic India/Capital Foods).
(c) Commodity environment largely benign near-term (tea, coffee).
(d) Pricing actions remain calibrated, with no broad-based hikes.
(e) Margin expansion trajectory remains intact (50–80bps improvement in FY27) despite near-term pressures, supported by mix, cost savings and operating leverage; ambition to reach 17%+ EBTIDA margin over the long-term.
Overall, management remains confident in sustaining strong growth momentum (continued scaling of the growth portfolio, robust innovation, and expanding distribution reach).
While near-term cost pressures persist, structural drivers for margin expansion remain intact

*CITI on Polycab*
Buy, TP Rs 10500
Management meet Key takeaways
(a) domestic demand has recovered after a weak March, which was impacted by geopolitical developments in West Asia; management saw healthy secondary sales from early April, with channel inventory at normalized levels;
(b) April/May saw mid- to high-single digit volume growth despite a high base (25%+ volume growth in 1QFY25);
(c) took another ~8–10% price hikes in 1Q to pass on the full imp act of higher commodity prices and currency depreciation;
(d) exports should grow faster than domestic in FY27, aided by US restart, higher enquiries and new geographies;
(e) EHV remains meaningful optionality, with ~Rs8bn capex broadly completed and potential revenue of ~Rs24bn at ~3x asset turn;
(f) C&W margins should remain near the higher end of 12–14%;
(g) BharatNet and RDSS combined will likely generate ~Rs18-20bn in revenue in FY27.

*CITI on RR Cable*
Buy, TP Rs 2650
Management meet Key takeaways
(a) demand continues to remain resilient – while export witnessed some disruption in March, April overall (domestic and export) was very strong, and management remains confident of double-digit volume growth in 1Q and FY27;
(b) have taken cumulative 25-30%+ price hike (in last 12 months) across Cables & Wires to pass on the full impact of higher commodity and currency depreciation;
(c) exports have rebounded sharply over the past 3–4 weeks, with management expecting both growth and margins to improve, aided by operating leverage;
(d) competition from new entrant should be manageable as wires/cables differ materially from paints — industry margins are lower, channel overlap is negligible with building material, approvals remain key for cables, and the market remains fragmented
(e) FMEG to breakeven in FY27, helped by strong traction in fans and pricing actions.

TCS TO TAKE ADDITIONAL $70 MN ( ₹ 662 Cr ) CHARGE IN DXC LAWSUIT ⚖️💻

• 🇺🇸 The U.S. Supreme Court has denied TCS' petition for review in the lawsuit filed by Computer Sciences Corporation (DXC Technology)

• 💰 TCS had already provided US$150 million for the matter in its books

• 📉 The company will now make an additional provision of US$70 million towards damages, interest and legal costs

• 📆 The additional charge will be recognized as a one-time exceptional expense in Q1 FY27

• ⚖️ The case pertains to litigation involving DXC Technology and follows prior disclosures made by TCS

• 🧾 The provision is expected to be accounted for in accordance with applicable accounting standards

🔴 Impact: Negative (The additional US$70 million provision will weigh on Q1 FY27 earnings, though it helps bring greater clarity and closure to the long-running legal dispute.)

GOLDMAN SACHS ON INDIA CONSUMER DURABLES

Maintained BUY on LG Electronics India and Havells; maintained SELL on Voltas

April and May volumes missed expectations, though full-quarter estimates remain intact; price increase expectations have been lowered

Expecting 16-29% year-on-year revenue growth across coverage for Q1 despite tempered pricing

Facing headwinds from raw material inflation, rupee depreciation, and operating deleverage that price hikes have not fully offset

El Niño-induced weak monsoons and shifts in Betu summer patterns raise uncertainty for Q1 and Q2 demand

Voltas remains highly sensitive to seasonality; LG and Havells are better protected by their diverse, non-cooling product portfolios

Nomura on Oil & Gas

Expect oil marketing companies, city gas distributors &  PLNG to benefit most from potential opening of the Strait of Hormuz
(SoH).

Upstream companies ONGC/Oil India  will likely face maximum negative impact due to lower realization on both oil and gas

Impact on GAIL will likely be moderately positive as upside from increased transmission volume will likely be partly offset due to lower

marketing margins & reduced margins on LPG production as the import parity price of LPG could come down.

Lastly, expect a moderate negative impact on Reliance due to lower refining GRMs as ~3mbpd of Middle East refining capacity may come back online.

Believe this will be partly offset by the higher volume of off-gas available for co’s petchem plants (as government may relax the gas pooling mechanism).

ARVIND SMARTSPACES ADDS ₹180 CR PROJECT IN SOUTH AHMEDABAD 🏡📍

• 🏗️ Arvind SmartSpaces has signed a new residential horizontal development project in South Ahmedabad

• 📐 The project spans 58.25 acres with a saleable area of 2.5 million sq. ft.

• 💰 The project carries an estimated top-line potential of ₹180 crore

• 🤝 The development is structured as a Joint Development Agreement (JDA)

• 🌆 Located on Kerala-Nalsarovar Road, the project targets the fast-growing plotted development and weekend homes segment

• 📈 South Ahmedabad is emerging as a promising micro-market, supported by improving infrastructure and rising demand for plotted developments

• 🏘️ The project strengthens Arvind SmartSpaces' presence in the horizontal real estate segment and expands its Ahmedabad portfolio

🟢 Impact: Positive (The project enhances future revenue visibility, expands the land bank through an asset-light model, and reinforces the company's growth pipeline.)

GOLD , METALS ; GOLDMAN SACHS EXPECTS FED TO SHIFT TO NEUTRAL TONE ON RATES 🟢

• 🇺🇸 Goldman Sachs expects the FOMC to adopt a more balanced policy stance by removing language on the "extent and timing of additional" rate adjustments

• 📊 The expected change suggests the Fed is moving away from a tightening bias and toward a more data-dependent approach

• 💼 The Fed continues to target maximum employment and 2% inflation over the long run

• ⚠️ The statement highlights Middle East tensions as a source of uncertainty for the economic outlook

• 📈 The Fed is likely to maintain rates while assessing incoming data, evolving outlook and balance of risks

• 🔍 Goldman also sees scope for the Fed to further simplify and shorten its policy statement

🟢 Impact: Positive for Global Equities (A less hawkish Fed stance can support risk assets, emerging markets and rate-sensitive sectors through lower bond yields and improved liquidity expectations.)
US Commerce Secretary Lutnick's letter to Anthropic flags the risk of latest Mythos and Fable models being diverted to foreign military intelligence users.

OIL INDIA SIGNS MoU WITH CSIR FOR ENERGY TECHNOLOGY R&D 🤝🔬

• 🧪 Oil India Ltd (OIL) has signed an MoU with CSIR for collaborative research, innovation and technology development in the energy sector

• ⚙️ The partnership will focus on upstream & downstream oil and gas operations, hydrocarbon reservoir optimisation and enhanced oil recovery (EOR)

• 🌱 Collaboration will also cover renewable energy, sustainable energy practices and critical minerals

• 💡 The tie-up aims to develop cutting-edge and cost-effective technologies to improve operational efficiency

• 🏭 The agreement strengthens OIL's technological capabilities through collaboration with India's premier research institution

• 🇮🇳 The initiative supports innovation-driven growth and contributes towards India's long-term energy security objectives

🟢 Impact: Positive (The collaboration can enhance OIL's R&D capabilities, improve operational efficiencies and strengthen its position in emerging energy technologies.)

NSE INDICES LAUNCHES 11 NEW SECTORAL INDICES 📊

• 🆕 NSE Indices has launched 11 new sectoral indices, taking the total number of sectoral indices to 34

• ⚡ New indices include:
Nifty Power
Nifty Capital Goods
Nifty Telecommunications
Nifty Construction
Nifty Consumer Services
Nifty Commercial & Transport Services
Nifty Retail
Nifty Hospitals
Nifty NBFC
Nifty Housing Finance
Nifty Insurance

• 📈 The new indices provide deeper and more granular sector representation across India’s economy

• 🏦 These indices are expected to serve as benchmarks for ETFs, index funds and structured products

• 💰 The launch could drive passive fund flows into constituent stocks of these sectors over time

• 🔍 The move expands investible opportunities for asset managers and enhances sector-specific investing

🟢 Impact: Positive (Sector-focused indices can attract ETF launches and passive inflows, benefiting constituent stocks across power, telecom, hospitals, NBFCs, insurance and other sectors.)

Adani Enterprises Ltd. & Jabil Inc. are targeting a strategic alliance to establish a world-class, vertically integrated AI & data center infrastructure manufacturing platform in India 🇮🇳.

This collaboration aims to leverage Jabil's extensive engineering & manufacturing expertise with Adani Group's vast infrastructure, green energy, & logistics network, along with its growing domestic data center operations. The partnership is set to address the surging demand for AI-ready data center hardware globally.

Key Pillars of the Manufacturing Platform:

- 𝗚𝗶𝗴𝗮-𝗦𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗥𝗮𝗰𝗸 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲: Plans to deploy multi-GW capacity for high-density AI Rack manufacturing in India, catering to global hyperscalers, co-location facilities, & enterprise data centers. This includes advanced manufacturing of next-gen liquid-cooled AI racks, servers, storage, & networking systems.
- 360-𝗗𝗲𝗴𝗿𝗲𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗜𝗻𝗳𝗿𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗘𝗰𝗼𝘀𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺: Encompasses manufacturing of white space & grey space devices such as Power Distribution Units (PDUs), Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), Transformers, Switchgears, Bus Bars, & advanced thermal management systems. The goal is to offer an end-to-end, design-to-deployment hardware ecosystem.
- 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁 𝗦𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 & 𝗧𝗿𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗼 𝗙𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗗𝗼𝗰𝘂𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: This initiative targets a global market opportunity exceeding USD 3 trillion over the next 7 years, driven by AI compute investments. Definitive operational frameworks & formal documentation are currently being developed.

𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗶𝗰 𝗥𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝗲:

Our talks covered cooperation in sectors like automobiles, railways, advanced manufacturing and green technology. 

The India-EU Free Trade Agreement will also add momentum to our partnership.

ANANT RAJ : INCORPORATES SINGAPORE CLOUD SUBSIDIARY 🌏☁️

• 🇸🇬 Anant Raj has successfully incorporated its wholly owned subsidiary Anant Raj Cloud Singapore Pte. Ltd. in Singapore on June 15, 2026. 

• ☁️ The subsidiary will focus on data centers, co-location and cloud services, including AI services. 

• 📡 The entity will provide cloud and AI services from Anant Raj’s upcoming data center infrastructure. 

• 💯 Anant Raj will hold 100% ownership in the Singapore entity through cash subscription. 

• 🌍 The move strengthens the company’s global presence and supports expansion in high-growth digital infrastructure businesses. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Anant Raj’s cloud and AI ambitions while expanding its international footprint in the fast-growing data center segment.)

NOUVEAU GLOBAL : SECURES ₹1.5 CR LOAN FOR FILM RIGHTS 🎬💰

• 🎬 Nouveau Global has entered into a ₹1.5 crore secured term loan agreement with Prime Capital Market Ltd. 

• 📽️ The funds will be used for acquisition of film rights and other business requirements of the company. 

• 🤝 The company has also approved a film rights purchase agreement worth around ₹1.5 crore. 

• 🏢 To secure the loan, the company will create a first pari-passu charge over certain immovable properties in Mumbai. 

• 🚫 The lender is not related to promoters, and the transaction is not a related-party deal. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Positive (The loan supports content acquisition and business expansion, but also increases leverage through secured borrowing.)

SIMCA ADVERTISING : SECURES ₹15.26 CR FY27 BUSINESS, BOOSTS REVENUE VISIBILITY 📺📈

• 💰 SIMCA has already secured business worth ~₹15.26 crore in FY27 from a leading financial services client, providing strong revenue visibility. 

• 🤝 The company continues to retain marquee clients across BFSI, real estate, FMCG and retail sectors. 

• 🏢 SIMCA currently serves 124 clients through a portfolio of 210 media assets, including 74 traditional hoardings and 136 digital billboard (DOOH) slots. 

• 📈 FY26 Revenue from Operations grew 74.7% YoY to ₹127.22 crore, while PAT surged 125.3% YoY to ₹16.62 crore. 

• ⚡ EBITDA increased 129.6% YoY to ₹23.55 crore, driven by operating leverage and higher digital media contribution. 

• 🚀 The company completed its IPO in FY26 and continues to expand its premium media network across India. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong client retention, recurring business and robust financial growth provide healthy earnings visibility for FY27.)

KUNDAN EDIFICE : RECEIVES ₹3.3 CR ORDER SCHEDULE FROM HAVELLS 💡📦

• 💰 Kundan Edifice has received confirmed order schedules worth ~₹3.30 crore from Havells India for supplies during June 2026. 

• 🔌 The orders comprise the company’s LV Flex (Strip Lights) and HV Flex (Rope Lights) product categories. 

• 🏭 The schedules provide better production planning and execution visibility for the company. 

• 🤝 The order reflects Kundan Edifice’s continued business engagement with one of India’s leading electrical and lighting brands. 

• 📈 The development reinforces the company’s presence in the lighting segment and highlights continued customer acceptance. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Repeat business from Havells strengthens revenue visibility and validates the company’s product portfolio.)

FABTECH TECHNOLOGIES : BAGS ₹31 CR INTERNATIONAL ORDER 🌍🏭

• 🌍 Fabtech Technologies has secured an international order from Botswana Vaccine Institute Limited. 

• 🏗️ The order involves design, supply, installation, testing and commissioning of HVAC and Clean Room systems on a turnkey basis. 

• 💉 The project includes clean rooms, HVAC, blending and filling project equipment for vaccine manufacturing infrastructure. 

• 💰 Total order value stands at ~₹31.23 crore, including Clean Room and HVAC components. 

• ⏳ The project is scheduled to be executed within 14 months. 

• 🚫 The contract is not a related-party transaction and promoters have no interest in the awarding entity. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (International order strengthens Fabtech’s export presence and enhances revenue visibility in the pharma infrastructure segment.)

MACH TRAVEL : EMPANELLED BY IRCTC FOR TOURISM SERVICES 🚆🌍

• 🚆 Mach Travel Solutions has been empanelled by IRCTC as a service provider for Category ‘A’ Tourist Packages across India. 

• 🏕️ The company can now participate in Swadesh Darshan tours, charter trains, rail tours, holiday packages and customized tours. 

• 🏨 Scope includes accommodation, transportation, catering, tour guides, customer support and destination logistics. 

• 📈 The empanelment strengthens Mach’s Government & Institutional Projects vertical and expands its presence in India’s growing tourism ecosystem. 

• 👥 The company has expanded its workforce 5x to over 360 employees as of May 2026 to support growth across travel segments. 

• 🇮🇳 Rising domestic tourism demand and improving connectivity provide a large opportunity for organized travel services. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (IRCTC empanelment enhances business opportunities, strengthens institutional presence and can drive future revenue growth.)

CRIZAC : PARTNERS WITH FOREIGNADMITS TO BUILD AI-LED STUDENT PLATFORM 🎓🤖

• 🌍 Crizac has made a strategic investment in ForeignAdmits, an AI-first student mobility platform serving 100,000+ students across 15+ countries. 

• 💰 ForeignAdmits has facilitated over ₹1,500 crore in education loans and works with 800+ partner consultancies. 

• 🤖 The partnership adds AI-powered tools such as LoanMonk for pre-admission loan eligibility and VisaMonk for visa interview preparation. 

• 🎯 Crizac aims to become a full-stack student mobility platform, covering admissions, financing and visa services end-to-end. 

• 👨‍💼 ForeignAdmits Founder Nikhil Jain will join Crizac as Chief Product & Marketing Officer to lead product strategy and consumer brand. 

• 🏫 Crizac already connects a global network of counsellors with 400+ institutions across the UK, Canada, Ireland, Australia and New Zealand. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition strengthens Crizac's AI capabilities, expands its service offerings and enhances its leadership in international student mobility.)

VIKRAN ENGINEERING : INDIA RATINGS ASSIGNS BBB+ RATING 📊

• 🏦 The company has received a credit rating of IND BBB+/Stable from India Ratings & Research. 

• 💰 The rating has been assigned for proposed non-convertible debentures (NCDs) worth ₹500 million (₹50 crore). 

• 📈 A stable outlook reflects expectations of steady business performance and credit profile. 

• 🔄 The rating could support future fundraising and improve access to debt markets. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Credit rating assignment enhances funding flexibility and strengthens investor confidence.)

PBM POLYTEX: STRIKE CALLED OFF, OPERATIONS RESUME 🏭

• 🧵 The illegal sit-down strike at the company's BLP Super Spinners unit, which began on June 2, 2026, has been called off. 

• ▶️ The company has confirmed that full operations resumed on June 15, 2026. 

• ⚙️ Resumption of production removes operational disruptions and restores normal manufacturing activity. 

• 📈 The development is expected to support production volumes and business continuity going forward. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Resumption of operations after the strike reduces uncertainty and supports revenue recovery.)

SUDARSHAN CHEMICAL INAUGURATES SECOND GLOBAL HQ IN FRANKFURT 🌍🎨

• 🇩🇪 Sudarshan Chemical has inaugurated its second global head office in Frankfurt, Germany

• 🏢 The new 21,000 sq. ft. office is located near Frankfurt and complements its existing global HQ in Pune

• 🤝 The move follows the company’s acquisition of Heubach Group in March 2025, supporting post-integration growth

• 🌐 The company now operates across 120+ countries, with 19 manufacturing sites and 4,000+ global customers

• 📍 The Frankfurt office is aimed at enhancing collaboration and integration across global operations

• 🇪🇺 The expansion underscores Sudarshan’s long-term commitment to Germany and Europe

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens global presence, supports integration synergies from Heubach acquisition, and enhances international growth prospects.)

• ⏳ Execution timeline is 48 months

PATEL ENGINEERING BAGS ₹126 CR IRRIGATION PROJECT IN MAHARASHTRA 💧🏗️

• 🚜 Patel Engineering has received an LOA for the Tasgaon Lift Irrigation Scheme from Maharashtra Krishna Valley Development Corporation. 

• 💰 The project has a total value of ₹126.37 crore, with Patel Engineering’s share at ₹64.45 crore (51%). 

• 🌾 The project aims to strengthen irrigation infrastructure across 2,277 hectares spanning six villages in Satara district, Maharashtra. 

• 🏗️ Scope includes headworks, pump houses, switchyards, rising mains and pipeline distribution networks. 

• ⏳ The project is scheduled for completion within 48 months. 

• 📈 The award formalizes Patel Engineering’s earlier L1 status and marks the commencement of project execution. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens order book visibility and reinforces Patel Engineering’s presence in irrigation infrastructure.)

CREATIVE NEWTECH LAUNCHES GLOBAL CONSUMER BRAND WOZOYO 🌍📱

• 🚀 Creative Newtech has launched WOZOYO, a new global consumer technology brand under its Hong Kong subsidiary Secured Connection Ltd. 

• 🇯🇵 WOZOYO is positioned in the affordable-premium segment and is described as “Designed in Japan with love for the world.” 

• 🎧 The initial product lineup includes audio devices, chargers, laptop accessories, smart home products and air purifiers. 

• 🏭 The launch marks Creative Newtech’s strategic shift from technology distribution to proprietary brand ownership, enabling deeper participation across the value chain. 

• 🌐 The company plans to leverage its existing retail, e-commerce and enterprise distribution network built over three decades. 

• 📈 WOZOYO targets a large addressable market, with its focus categories representing a global opportunity exceeding US$213 billion. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Brand ownership can improve margins, create intellectual property and unlock long-term value beyond pure distribution.)

Bright Outdoor Media Limited Board Approves Migration to BSE & NSE Main Boards

The Board of Directors of Bright Outdoor Media Limited, at its meeting held on June 12, 2026, has approved the proposal for migration of the Company's equity shares from the BSE SME Platform to the Main Board of BSE Limited and the proposed listing on the Main Board of the National Stock Exchange of India Limited (NSE), subject to necessary regulatory and statutory approvals.

Bright Outdoor Media Limited was listed on the BSE SME Platform on March 24, 2023, through a successful IPO at an issue price of ₹146 per share, aggregating to approximately ₹55.48 Cr. Since its listing, the Company has continued to strengthen its presence across outdoor advertising, DOOH, transit advertising, railway and airport media formats, with an established footprint across Mumbai and Navi Mumbai.

The proposed Main Board migration marks an important milestone in the Company’s capital market journey and is expected to provide access to a broader investor base while enhancing the visibility and liquidity of its equity shares.

WAAREE RTL EXTENDS TIMELINE FOR APSPL ACQUISITION ⏳🔋

• 🏗️ Waaree Renewable Technologies has extended the completion timeline for the acquisition of Associated Power Structures Pvt Ltd (APSPL). 

• 📅 The acquisition was earlier targeted to close by June 15, 2026, but is now expected to be completed by June 30, 2026. 

• 📝 The delay is due to procedural requirements, including transfer of existing securities and fresh issuance/allotment of securities. 

• 🔄 The company clarified that only the completion timeline has changed, while the acquisition process remains on track. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The delay is procedural in nature and does not indicate any change in the strategic rationale of the acquisition.)

METHODHUB HOSTS AI ROADSHOW FOR MANUFACTURING 🚀🤖

• 🏭 MethodHub launched MFG.NXT, a CXO roadshow in Chennai and Bengaluru focused on AI-led transformation in manufacturing, pharma and logistics. 

• 🧠 The company showcased its BalinX AI platform along with solutions such as Factirize, CoAPP, QuantumHire, DataFoundry.ai and GreenFuturz. 

• ⚙️ AI use cases include smart factories, AI vision inspection, predictive analytics, digital documentation and intelligent hiring. 

• 💊 DataFoundry.ai targets pharma firms with AI-powered safety monitoring, compliance and pharmacovigilance solutions. 

• 📡 GreenFuturz integrates RFID, IoT and AI to enable real-time inventory visibility and asset tracking across factories and warehouses. 

• 🌍 MethodHub currently operates in the US, India, Canada and Thailand, with plans to expand into Mexico and Costa Rica. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens MethodHub's AI positioning, enhances enterprise visibility and could support future business opportunities across manufacturing and pharma sectors.)

UNITED DRILLING TOOLS SECURES RUSSIAN EXPORT ORDER 🇷🇺🛢️

• 📦 United Drilling Tools (UDTL) has received a trial export order from Trident East Limited, Russia, for the supply of PUMA Connectors. 

• 🏆 The company claims to be the only manufacturer in India producing these highly specialized connectors used in critical oil & gas applications. 

• 🌍 This marks the first-ever supply of PUMA Connectors from India to Russia, opening up opportunities in the Russian market. 

• 🤝 Successful execution of the trial order could lead to a long-term relationship and larger future orders from the customer. 

• ⏳ The order is expected to be executed within one month, subject to stringent quality standards. 

• 🔬 The order follows extensive technical evaluations, highlighting UDTL’s engineering and manufacturing capabilities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Entry into Russia for a niche product and potential for repeat export orders strengthen UDTL’s global footprint and growth prospects.)

APOLLO HOSPITALS PROVIDES CLARITY ON HEALTHCO DE-MERGER 🏥

• 🔄 Apollo Hospitals shared additional details on the proposed demerger and listing of Apollo Healthtech Ltd, highlighting governance and shareholder safeguards. 

• 👥 Apollo Healthtech’s board will have at least 50% independent directors, ensuring strong corporate governance. 

• 👩‍💼 Shobana Kamineni is proposed to be appointed as Executive Chairperson, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals. 

• 📉 Investor and promoter board nomination rights will lapse if their stake falls below 10%, with Articles to be amended accordingly. 

• 💰 The Upside Agreement is fully funded by investor Rasmeli and involves no cash outflow from Apollo Healthtech or other shareholders. It provides up to 9% of upside only upon achieving 4x MOIC. 

• 🗳️ The Upside Agreement will require approval from public shareholders both before and after listing of Apollo Healthtech. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Enhanced governance safeguards and clarity around the demerger strengthen investor confidence in Apollo Healthtech’s future listing.)

GABION TECHNOLOGIES WINS ₹6.6 CR ORDERS 🏗️

• 🇮🇳 All orders are domestic and received in the normal course of business, with no related-party transactions or promoter interest.

📢Digikore Studios Limited Receives NSE In-Principle Approval for Promoter Debt-to-Equity Conversion

About the Update
* Received NSE in-principle approval for conversion of ₹11 crore promoter funding into equity.
* Will allot up to 14.16 lakh shares at ₹77.70/share to Promoter & MD Abhishek More against unsecured loans already provided to the company.
* No fresh fund raise involved; this is a conversion of existing promoter loans into equity.

Post Conversion
* Transaction will strengthen the balance sheet, reduce liabilities, improve net worth, and enhance financial flexibility.
* Promoter & Promoter Group holding will increase from 58.46% to 62.64% , while management control remains unchanged.

_The move highlights the promoter's strong confidence in Digikore's long-term growth prospects and supports future expansion in global VFX and digital content markets._

TILAKNAGAR INDUSTRIES BRANDS EARN GLOBAL RECOGNITION 🌍🥃

• 🌎 Courrier Napoleon Brandy ranked as the 8th-largest brandy brand globally, with sales of 1.5 million cases. 

• 🥃 Imperial Blue Whisky, acquired in December 2025, is now the 9th-largest spirits brand globally and recorded its highest-ever monthly sales in May 2026 under TI ownership. 

• 📈 Tilaknagar Industries achieved its highest-ever monthly sales in May 2026, highlighting strong portfolio execution and market expansion.

CRIZAC ACQUIRES 37.4% STAKE IN EDTECH PLATFORM 🎓🤖

• 💰 Crizac will invest ₹1.25 crore in Edument Consultancy Pvt Ltd through acquisition of CCPS and CCDs from existing investors. 

• 🤝 Upon conversion, Crizac will hold 37.41% of the fully diluted share capital of Edument. 

• 🧠 Edument operates an AI-powered B2B SaaS platform for study-abroad services, including admissions, education loans and visa readiness. It serves 700+ counseling partners and 200+ university partners across 5+ countries. 

• 🌍 The investment aims to strengthen Crizac's offerings in AI-led student mobility infrastructure and cross-border education financing. 

• 📅 The acquisition is expected to be completed by 12 August 2026 and is not a related-party transaction. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic investment expands Crizac’s capabilities in AI-driven education services and creates cross-selling opportunities.)

• 🌾 The projects cover an aggregate area of 680 hectares (Ha). 

• 💰 The total order value stands at ₹17.15 crore (including GST) for all four contracts. 

• ⏳ The contracts are to be executed within 11 months from the date of site handover.

CLSA on Cement sector

Post correction over past three months, Indian cement stocks are now trading at c. 1SD below the historical median and implying no profitability improvement even in FY28 (vs exit FY26). 

While believe FY27 is likely to be soft due to cost pressures and muted demand growth, as war risk subsides, expect profitability to jump in FY28

See value across most stocks in sector but prefer Ultratech, Dalmia and Shree given a better growth outlook.

Maintain Overweight

Analyst Top Pick

Maintain target price 1,803

Energy security policies and tighter refining markets seen keeping product spreads structurally stronger for longer

Oil to chemicals earnings supported despite higher logistics costs; company well placed on heavy and sour crude processing and diversified sourcing

Chemical cycle recovery aided by advantaged feedstocks through US ethane and captive naphtha expected to lift earnings 6-8% this year

Monetisation 4.0 underway as solar module, cell and restorage manufacturing ramps up; not fully reflected in

MS on Meesho

Bernstein on Aurobindo Pharma

Believe this is yet another evidence of poor quality execution by Aurobindo

UBS on Suzlon

Buy, TP Rs 72

Investor day takeaways,

Management highlighted a clear strategic pivot to an integrated renewable energy (RE) platform targeting installed base share of 40% in five years in wind (vs 33% currently)

As compared to solar manufacturing peers, Suzlon’s integrated RE strategy is relatively asset-light whereby it intends to leverage on upcoming robust integrated solar manufacturing capacities via strategic tie ups

EPC & DevCo model will ensure reduced risk and better predictability/control in projects while delivering wind farms to customers.

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3

Nomura on DR Reddys

Buy, TP Rs 1600

Announced launch of gBosulif (Bosutinib tabs) 400 mg in US, with 180- days exclusivity

Product has annual sales of USD214mn at company level

As per IQVIA, product experienced volume growth of 6% y-y in MAT April 2026.

DRRD has launched the product in partnership with MSN (unlisted), which is sole first ANDA filer for product

Launch is a positive surprise, as partnership with MSN was not disclosed earlier.

Assuming 30-40% price erosion during the exclusivity period and a 40% market share, expect product to contribute $25-30mn in revenue in FY26F

Nomura on HDFC BK

Buy, TP Rs 950

Market concerns that have weighed on HDFCB are not independent problems.

Deposit growth lagging expectations, CD ratio above management's guidance, LCR lowest among large private peers, & a higher reliance on short-term wholesale funding

They are same problems viewed from different perspectives

RBI's FCNR (B) scheme could address all these in a single window, making HDFCB a potential standout beneficiary

Bank could garner 15% of overall FCNR flows, – equivalent to 3% of its current deposit base

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3

Jefferies on Telecom

During FY26, sector revenues rose 10% YoY to a record US$32bn, led by ARPU growth and strong momentum in B- and C-Circles.

Bharti Airtel led sector gains, adding revenue market share across most circles, supported by premiumization and rural expansion.

VIL’s revenue rose 5% YoY, but subscriber losses drove further share erosion.

Over FY26-28E, expect revenues to grow at 13% CAGR to US$41bn, assuming a 15% tariff hike in Dec-26.
